program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimleri ve yazılım mühendisliği disiplinine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimi; bu alanlardaki kuramsal ve uygulamalı bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinde kullanabilme becerisini kazanır.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi; bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisini edinir.

Karmaşık bir sistemi, süreci, cihazı veya ürünü gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak şekilde tasarlama becerisi; bu amaçla modern tasarım yöntemlerini uygulama becerisini kazanır.

Yazılım mühendisliği uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için gerekli olan modern teknik ve araçları seçme ve kullanma becerisi; bilişim teknolojilerini etkin bir şekilde kullanma becerisini gösterir.

Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya yazılım mühendisliği disiplinine özgü araştırma konularının incelenmesi için, deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisini geliştirir.
Yazlım mühendisliği ile ilgili alanlarda disiplin içi ve çok disiplinli takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisi; bireysel çalışma becerisi gösterir.

Türkçe sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi; etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisi gösterir.

En az bir yabancı dil bilgisi alır; etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisi kazanır.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği knousnda farkındalık; bilgiye erişebilme, bilim ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleme ve kendini sürekli yenileme becerisini edinir.
Etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk hakkında bilgi sahip olur.
Yazılım mühendisliği uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatı uygulamaları hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Girişimcilik ve yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık kazanır.
Sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Yazılım mühendisliği uygulamalarının evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki etkileri ve çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında bilgi sahibi olur.
Mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalık kazanır.

Yazılım alternatiflerini irdeleyerek bilgisayar tabanlı sistemlerin modellenmesi ve tasarımında, algoritma prensiplerini, matematiksel temelleri ve bilgisayar bilimleri teorisini uygulama becerisini uygular.
Yazılım sistemlerinin analiz, tasarım, uygulama, doğrulama, geçerleme ve bakım süreçlerini uygulayarak geliştirilmesinde mühendislik yaklaşımlarını uygulama becerisi kazanır.

courses.name: General Physics I
courses.purpose: The main purpose of this course is to reveal the relationship between mathematics, physics, and engineering sciences in the light of the fundamental principles of mechanics based on mathematics, and to apply the basic principles of physics to engineering fields.
courses.description: Measurement, rectilinear motion, vectors, motion in two and three dimensions, force and motion I, force and motion II, kinetic energy and work, center of mass and linear momentum, rotation, rolling, torque and angular momentum, equilibrium and elasticity.
learning_outcomes: Understands mechanics problems and their applications to better understand the related engineering disciplines.
Comprehends the conceptual understanding of mechanics topics and their application to engineering problems.
Integrates the principles of basic sciences and engineering sciences and demonstrates their common applications.
Increases problem-solving skills and motivation.
Establishes a conceptual relationship between mechanics and related engineering sciences.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Motion in Two and Three Dimensions; Vectors
Hafta 5: Motion in Two and Three Dimensions; Vectors
Hafta 6: Newton's Laws of Motion, First Midterm Exam
Hafta 7: Applications of Newton's Laws: Friction, Circular Motion
Hafta 8: Applications of Newton's Laws: Friction, Circular Motion
Hafta 12: Rotational Motion, Second Midterm Exam
resources: Physics for Scientists & Engineers with Modern Physics, Douglas C. Giancoli (5th edition), Pearson (2023)

courses.name: Calculus I
courses.purpose: This course aims to fill gaps in students' high school mathematics background and equip them with the ability to solve engineering problems using differential and integral calculus of one variable.
courses.description: Basic concepts, limits and continuity, derivatives, applications of derivatives, L'Hopital's rule, integrals, applications of integrals, integrals and transcendental functions, integration techniques, improper integrals, sequences.
learning_outcomes: Understands the concept of function, defines and uses functions, and represents functions graphically.
Understands the concepts of limit and continuity.
Understands the concept of derivative and computes derivatives of single-variable functions.
Solves maximum, minimum, and related rates problems using derivatives.
Understands the concept of integral and uses integration techniques in solving area, volume, and other problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: P.1 Real numbers and the real number line, P.2 Cartesian coordinates in the plane, P.3 Graphs of quadratic equations, P.4 Functions and their graphs
Hafta 2: P.5 Composite functions, P.6 Polynomials and rational functions, P.7 Trigonometric functions
Hafta 3: 1.1 Examples of rates of change and areas, 1.2 Limits of functions, 1.3 Infinite limits and limits at infinity, 1.4 Continuity
Hafta 4: 1.5 Precise definition of a limit, 2.1 Tangent lines and their slopes, 2.1 Derivative, 2.3 Rules of differentiation
Hafta 5: 2.4 Chain rule, 2.5 Derivatives of trigonometric functions, 2.6 Higher-order derivatives
Hafta 6: 2.7 Differentials and applications of derivatives, 2.8 Mean Value Theorem, 2.9 Implicit differentiation, 3.1 Inverse functions
Hafta 8: 3.2 Exponential and logarithmic functions, 3.3 Natural logarithm and exponential function, 3.4 Growth and decay (Theorems 4, 5 and 6 and examples), 3.5 Inverse trigonometric functions
Hafta 9: 3.6 Hyperbolic functions (definitions and derivatives only), 4.1 Related rates, 4.3 Indeterminate forms
Hafta 10: 4.4 Extreme values of functions, 4.5 Concavity and inflection points, 4.6 Graphing a function
Hafta 11: 4.8 Optimization problems, 4.9 Linear approximations, 2.10 Antiderivatives and initial value problems (antiderivatives and indefinite integral), 5.1 Summation and sigma notation
Hafta 12: 5.2 Area as a limit of sums, 5.3 Definite integral, 5.4 Properties of the definite integral, 5.5 Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
Hafta 13: 5.6 Substitution rule, 5.7 Areas of plane regions, 6.1 Integration by parts
Hafta 14: 6.2 Integrals of rational functions, 6.3 Trigonometric substitution, 6.5 Improper integrals
Hafta 15: 7.1 Volumes by slicing method – solids of revolution, 7.2 Volumes by slicing method, 7.3 Lengths of plane curves and surface area (plane curve lengths only)
resources: Calculus: A Complete Course, R. A. Adams, C. Essex, 7th Edition; Pearson Addison Wesley

courses.name: Fundamentals of Computing
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the engineering profession, job opportunities, and ethical issues in computing. It also provides a general overview of computer science by introducing basic computer architecture, machine language, data representation, operating systems, networks and the Internet, and programming languages.
courses.description: Engineering fundamentals, computer engineering as a profession, job opportunities, professional organizations in the field of computer engineering, ethical issues in computing, hardware components of a computer system, data representation and machine language commands, coordinating background activities of a computer using an operating system, and the basics of networks and the Internet.
learning_outcomes: Define different aspects of Computer, Software and Information Systems Engineering
Identify ethical issues in computing
Describe the internal structure of a computer and data representation
Construct simple machine language instructions for problem solving
Explain the functions of operating systems and networks
Classify programming languages
Describe network strategies and Internet usage

Hafta 11: Basics of Networks and the Internet
Hafta 12: Basics of Networks and the Internet

courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with the fundamental concepts and principles of chemistry and to build a basis for understanding chemical phenomena in engineering and science contexts.
courses.description: Matter and measurement; atoms, molecules and ions; stoichiometry: calculations involving chemical formulas and equations; oxidation-reduction reactions; thermochemistry; electronic structure of atoms; periodic properties of elements; basic concepts of chemical bonding; molecular geometry and bonding theories; gases; intermolecular forces, liquids and solids; chemical kinetics; chemical thermodynamics; electrochemistry.

courses.name: Career Planning
courses.purpose: This course aims to help students gain awareness of their interests, personal characteristics, and values so that they can plan a career aligned with their future goals.
courses.description: Atılım identity, student-oriented programs, faculty and department introductions, career planning and cooperative education, Erasmus, CV writing types, interview techniques, effective communication and presentation techniques.
learning_outcomes: Recognition of Career Centers and Their Activities: Ensuring that the student is informed about the services offered by the Career Center and establishing a connection between the Career Center and the student.
Increasing Self-Awareness: The student learns concepts such as intelligence, personality, knowledge, skills, abilities, and competencies and relates these concepts to career.

Exploring Career Options: Students become familiar with sectors such as the public sector, private sector, academia, and non-governmental organizations, understand the differences among sectors, and observe working life in these sectors.
Developing Soft Skills: Increasing awareness of the importance of developing soft skills in the career process.
Exploring Activities That Can Contribute to Career Planning: Informing students about activities outside their courses that can contribute to their careers throughout university life.

Recognition of International Exchange Programs: Students gain awareness of the contributions of international exchange programs to personal development and academic life, and their positive reflections on career plans.

Recognition of Yetenek Kapısı: The student learns that counseling can be obtained from Career Centers through Yetenek Kapısı and that applications can be made for fairs, seminars, workshops, and internship announcements.

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkılâp Tarihi I
courses.purpose: Öğrencilere vatan sevgisini aşılamak, Atatürk ilkelerine bağlı, Atatürkçü gençler olarak yetişmelerini sağlamak, demokrasi bilincini geliştirerek çağdaş demokratik yönetim düzeyine hangi aşamalardan geçilerek ulaşıldığını kavratmaktır. Öğrencilere, günümüzün sorunlarını Atatürkçü yaklaşımla çözüm önerileri geliştirecek tutum, davranış ve yeteneği kazandırmaktır.

Önemli ekonomik kararları ve sonuçlarını bilir.
Türk demokrasi tarihinin gelişim çizgisini bilir.
Türk Anayasa metinlerinin genel hatlarıyla bilir.
Balkan Savaşı ve Birinci Dünya Savaşının nedenlerini ve sonuçlarını bilir.
Milli Mücadelenin hangi koşullarda, nasıl kazanıldığını bilir.
Lozan Anlaşmasının içeriğini ve anlamını bilir.

courses.name: Introduction to Software Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce computer software and its types, the fundamental concepts of the software engineering discipline, various software process models, the incremental software development approach, related activities, tools and techniques used, and software project, configuration, and quality management.
courses.description: Computer software and its types, the software engineering discipline and its fundamental concepts, software process models, requirements engineering concepts, system modeling, architectural design, design and implementation, software testing, software evolution and maintenance, project management, quality and configuration management.
learning_outcomes: Define and distinguish various software engineering concepts, and understand software types and the fundamental concepts of the discipline.
Explain the basics of software project management and risk management as part of planning activities.
Apply requirements collection and classification principles by creating and categorizing different types of software requirements such as functional and non-functional requirements.
Understand software development stages and the use of UML diagrams in analysis and design to define relationships, interactions, and structures within software elements.
Identify software testing levels and appreciate the activities at each level.
Understand the importance of software maintenance activities in SDLC and its types.
Understand and compare different software development methodologies.

Hafta 3: Project Management and Planning (Lab: Drawing PERT and Gantt Charts with a CASE tool)

Hafta 7: Low-Level Software Design (Drawing Activity diagrams with a CASE tool)
Hafta 12: Process Models (Lab: Preparing a prototype with a CASE tool)

courses.name: Atatürk İlkeleri ve İnkilâp Tarihi II
courses.purpose: Öğrencilere vatan sevgisini aşılamak; Atatürk ilkelerine bağlı, Atatürkçü gençler olarak yetişmelerini sağlamak; demokrasi bilincini geliştirerek çağdaş demokratik yönetim düzeyine hangi aşamalardan geçilerek ulaşıldığını kavratmaktır. Öğrencilere, günümüzün sorunlarını Atatürkçü yaklaşımla çözüm önerileri geliştirecek tutum, davranış ve yeteneği kazandırmaktır.
courses.description: Kurtuluş Savaşı; Lozan Antlaşması; Cumhuriyetin ilanı; saltanatın ve halifeliğin kaldırılması; Atatürk devrimleri; milli ekonominin kurulması; II. Dünya Savaşı, öncesi ve sonrası; 1960 sonrası Türkiye Cumhuriyeti.
learning_outcomes: Türkiye Cumhuriyetinin ilanından günümüze kadar olan süreçteki siyasi olayları neden-sonuç ilişkisi içerisinde anlar
Önemli ekonomik kararları ve sonuçlarını bilir
Türk demokrasi tarihinin gelişim çizgisini bilir
Türk Anayasa metinlerini genel hatlarıyla bilir
İkinci Dünya Savaşının nedenlerini ve sonuçlarını bilir
Atatürk ilkelerini bilir
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Saltanatın kaldırılması, Cumhuriyetin ilanı, Halifeliğin kaldırılması, Terakkiperver Cumhuriyet Fırkası, 20 Ocak 1921 Anayasası.
Hafta 2: Şeyh Sait isyanı, 20 Nisan 1924 Anayasası, İzmir suikastı, Serbest Cumhuriyet Fırkası, Menemen olayı.
Hafta 3: Hukuk, eğitim ve kültür, ekonomi ve günlük hayata ilişkin devrimler, düzenlemeler.
Hafta 4: Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin 1923-1930 Dönemi: Türk-Yunan ilişkileri, Musul sorunu, Türk-Fransız ilişkileri.
Hafta 5: Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin 1923-1930 Dönemi: Türk-İtalyan ilişkileri, Türk-Sovyet ilişkileri, Doğu devletleri ile ilişkiler.
Hafta 6: Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin 1931-1939 Dönemi: Türkiye’nin Milletler Cemiyetine girişi, Türk-Alman ilişkileri, Balkan Antantı, Türk-İngiliz İlişkileri.
Hafta 8: Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin 1931-1939 Dönemi: Montreux Boğazlar Sözleşmesi, Sadabat Paktı, Hatay sorunu.
Hafta 9: Atatürk İlkeleri: Cumhuriyetçilik, Halkçılık, Milliyetçilik.
Hafta 10: Atatürk ilkeleri: Devletçilik, Laiklik, İnkılapçılık.
Hafta 11: İsmet İnönü Dönemi (1938-1950). İkinci Dünya Savaşı.

courses.name: Calculus II
courses.purpose: This course is designed as a continuation of MATH151 Calculus I. Its purpose is to equip students with the skills needed to solve engineering problems using series, analytic geometry, and multivariable differentiation and integration.
courses.description: Infinite series, vectors in the plane and polar functions, vectors in space and motion, multivariable functions and their derivatives, multiple integrals: double integrals, area calculations, double integrals in polar coordinates, triple integrals in Cartesian, cylindrical and spherical coordinates, and line integrals.
learning_outcomes: understand and use sequences, infinite series, power series of functions, and Taylor and Maclaurin series
use vectors in analytic geometry and interpret lines, planes, and surfaces in three-dimensional space
understand and use multivariable functions, partial derivatives, directional derivatives, gradient vectors, and tangent planes
find local and absolute extrema of multivariable functions, use Lagrange multipliers, and solve optimization problems
understand and use double and triple integrals in different coordinate systems
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: 9.1 Sequences and Convergence, 9.2 Infinite Series
Hafta 2: 9.3 Convergence for Positive Series (Integral Test, Comparison Tests, Ratio and Root Tests), 9.4 Absolute and Conditional Convergence
Hafta 3: 9.5 Power Series, 9.6 Taylor and Maclaurin Series (Convergence of Taylor Series; Error Analysis)
Hafta 4: 9.7 Applications of Taylor and Maclaurin Series, 10.1 Analytic Geometry in Three-Dimensional Coordinate System
Hafta 5: 10.2 Vectors, 10.3 Vector (Cross) Product in Three-Dimensional Space
Hafta 6: 10.4 Planes and Lines, 10.5 Quadratic Surfaces
Hafta 8: 12.1 Multivariable Functions, 12.2 Limits and Continuity
Hafta 9: 12.3 Partial Derivatives, 12.4 Higher-Order Derivatives, 12.5 Chain Rule
Hafta 10: 12.6 Linear Approximations, Differentiability and Differential, 12.7 Gradient and Directional Derivatives, 12.8 Implicit Functions
Hafta 11: 13.1 Extrema, 13.2 Extrema of a Function Defined on a Bounded Region
Hafta 13: 14.2 Iterated Multiple Integral in Cartesian Coordinates, 14.4 Multiple Integral in Polar Coordinates
Hafta 14: 14.5 Triple Integrals, 14.6 Change of Variables in Triple Integrals (Cylindrical and Spherical Coordinates)
Hafta 15: 14.6 Change of Variables in Triple Integrals (Cylindrical and Spherical Coordinates)

courses.name: Object Oriented Programming
courses.purpose: This course teaches how class objects are abstracted through their properties and behaviors. Students learn to model real-world objects and apply the three important components of object-oriented programming: encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism using UML and C++.
courses.description: Data types, expressions and statements, functions and scope rules, class definition, inheritance, polymorphism, name overloading, templates, exception handling, input/output, and introduction to object-oriented concepts using UML and the C++ language.
learning_outcomes: Explain the programming method that models the real world using UML
Apply encapsulation, inheritance, and polymorphism concepts
Use function and operator overloading
Use exception handling
Apply function and class templates
Use object-oriented methodology in programming
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to object-oriented programming (with UML)
Hafta 3: Transition from C to C++

courses.name: Software Requirements Engineering
courses.purpose: This course aims to cover the most up-to-date processes, methods, and techniques in software requirements engineering. It also provides the detailed knowledge and skills needed to define high-quality software requirements, which are vital in software development projects.
courses.description: Fundamentals of software requirements, requirements from the customer perspective, requirements engineering practices, the role of the requirements analyst, developing product vision and project scope, understanding customer and user requirements, documenting requirements, risk reduction through prototyping, prioritizing requirements, and requirements management.
learning_outcomes: Gain skill in defining high-quality software requirements.
Acquire knowledge about requirements-related methodologies and techniques that can be applied immediately to a wide range of systems.
Use methods, tools, and techniques in requirements engineering.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Software process and software life cycle
Hafta 12: Requirements management, requirements tracing and change management

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: This course is designed to enrich engineering students' knowledge of linear algebra and to teach the fundamentals and applications of methods for solving systems of linear equations that arise in engineering problem solving.
courses.description: Linear equations and matrices, real vector spaces, inner product spaces, linear transformations and matrices, determinants, eigenvalues and eigenvectors.
learning_outcomes: understands the concept of matrices, performs algebraic operations on matrices, finds inverses of invertible matrices, solves systems of linear equations using echelon-type matrices, and determines whether a solution exists, is unique, or has infinitely many solutions
understands and applies vector spaces and subspaces, linear independence, basis, dimension, and the rank of a matrix
understands and uses inner product, the Gram-Schmidt process, and orthogonal complement concepts
understands and uses linear transformations and the matrices of linear transformations
computes determinants and solves systems with a unique solution using determinants (Cramer's Rule)
understands eigenvalues and eigenvectors, determines whether matrices are diagonalizable, and performs diagonalization

Hafta 7: Spanning, Linear Independence, Basis and Dimension
Hafta 8: Homogeneous Systems, Coordinates and Isomorphism, Rank of a Matrix
Hafta 9: Inner Product Spaces, Gram-Schmidt Method
Hafta 10: Orthogonal Complements, Linear Transformations and Matrices
Hafta 11: Kernel and Image Space of a Linear Transformation
Hafta 12: Matrix of a Linear Transformation
Hafta 14: Diagonalization and Similar Matrices, Diagonalization of Symmetric Matrices

courses.name: Discrete Computational Structures
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the mathematical concepts that are fundamental to computer science.
courses.description: Fundamental mathematical objects of computational mathematics: set calculus; series, relations, functions and partitions; deductive mathematical logic and proof techniques; discrete number systems; inference and recursion; graphs and subgraphs; tree structures; planarity of graphs; covering problems; path problems; directed graphs.
learning_outcomes: Apply mathematical reasoning, combinatorial analysis, and discrete structures.
Understand discrete structures and algorithmic thinking.
Apply mathematical thinking skills.
Formulate problems using mathematical structures.

courses.name: Human Computer Interaction
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to provide students with an overview of user-centered design and development tools in order to help them develop more effective user interfaces.
courses.description: User interface basic principles, human capabilities and limitations, usability models and principles, user and task analysis, design, prototype development and evaluation, color and typography, new user interface techniques.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic terms and concepts related to human-computer interaction
Define human capabilities and limitations
Design user interfaces, develop prototypes, and conduct usability testing
Be informed about emerging user interface technologies such as accessibility, augmented reality, and virtual reality

Hafta 2: How the Human Brain Works I

Hafta 12: Innovative Technologies (Wearable Technologies, AR & VR)

courses.name: Probability and Statistics
courses.purpose: To help students learn the fundamental probability and statistics concepts needed to solve practical engineering problems that require statistical techniques.
courses.description: Introduction to probability and statistics, random variables and probability distributions, expected value, sampling distributions, one- and two-sample estimation problems, hypothesis testing, and simple linear regression.
learning_outcomes: Understand the basic concepts of probability theory and use them in engineering problems.
Understand the applications of statistics in different engineering fields and improve them.
Evaluate and solve real-life processes and problems using statistical applications.
Perform statistical analyses using an appropriate statistical package program.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The scope of probability and statistics in engineering

courses.name: Differential Equations
courses.purpose: This course is designed for engineering students because differential equations are applicable in many scientific fields. The aim of the course is to provide understanding of ordinary differential equations and systems of ordinary differential equations, and to give methods for their solutions. It also provides preliminary knowledge about partial differential equations.
courses.description: First-order and higher-order linear ordinary differential equations, series solutions of differential equations, Laplace transforms, systems of linear ordinary differential equations, Fourier analysis, and partial differential equations.
learning_outcomes: Decide on the existence and uniqueness of a solution and choose an appropriate method to find the solution.
Use appropriate methods to solve first-, second-, and higher-order ordinary differential equations.
Solve a differential equation using power series and Laplace transform methods.
Solve systems of linear ordinary differential equations by elimination and Laplace transform methods.
Find Fourier series expansions of periodic functions.
Solve some basic partial differential equations such as the heat, wave, and Laplace equations by the method of separation of variables.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: First-order ordinary differential equations: basic concepts.
Hafta 2: Solutions, existence-uniqueness theorem, separable equations, linear equations.
Hafta 3: Bernoulli equations, homogeneous equations, exact differential equations and integrating factors.
Hafta 4: Transformations, higher-order linear ordinary differential equations: basic theory of higher-order linear differential equations.
Hafta 5: Reduction of order method, homogeneous constant coefficient equations.
Hafta 6: Method of undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters method.
Hafta 8: Cauchy-Euler equations, series solutions of ordinary differential equations: power series solutions (ordinary point).
Hafta 9: Power series solutions (ordinary point) continued, power series solutions (regular-singular point).
Hafta 10: Laplace transforms: basic properties of Laplace transforms, convolution.
Hafta 11: Solving differential equations by means of Laplace transforms.
Hafta 12: Systems of linear ordinary differential equations: solving systems of linear ordinary differential equations by the elimination method.
Hafta 13: Solving systems of linear ordinary differential equations using Laplace transforms.
Hafta 14: Fourier analysis: even and odd functions, periodic functions, trigonometric series, Fourier series and Fourier sine and cosine series for arbitrary periodic functions.
Hafta 15: Partial differential equations: separation of variables method, solution of the heat, wave and Laplace equations.
resources: Lectures on Differential Equations, E. Akyıldız, Y. Akyıldız, Ş. Alpay, A. Erkip and A. Yazıcı, Matematik Vakfı Yayın No:1
Differential Equations, 2nd Edition, Shepley L. Ross, John Wiley and Sons, 1984.
Advanced Engineering Mathematics, 8th Edition, Erwin Kreyszig, John Wiley and Sons, 1998.
Ordinary Differential Equations Problem Book with Solutions, Rajeh Eid, Atılım University Publications 16, Ankara, Atılım University, 2005.

courses.name: Academic English III
courses.purpose: The general aim of this course is to help students further develop their integrated academic reading, writing, listening, and speaking skills. It also aims to promote learner autonomy so that students can transfer these skills to their academic and professional lives. Students who successfully complete the course are expected to become Independent Users at B2 level according to the Common European Framework of Reference for Languages.
courses.description: The course includes integrated academic skills activities focusing on reading, writing, listening, and speaking. Students read various texts, listen to audio recordings, write reflective essays, participate in class discussions, and deliver poster presentations. They develop productive skills through interactive communication activities such as brainstorming, pair and group discussions, peer feedback, and written and oral tasks. Based on current and authentic materials, the work is carried out through a digital platform that offers various engaging interactive tools such as surveys, polls, and link-based activities.
learning_outcomes: Apply various reading strategies to academic texts in order to identify and evaluate main ideas and supporting details.
Distinguish between explicit and implicit meanings.
Extract and synthesize relevant information from different sources.
Identify main ideas and supporting details in audio or video recordings.
Critically evaluate and respond appropriately to listened content.
Express and support opinions clearly and confidently in discussions using appropriate content and language.
Prepare and deliver a clear, well-structured poster presentation using language accurately and appropriately.
Analyze sample reflective essays to identify their key features.
Write a clear, well-organized reflective essay with appropriate content and correct language use.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction and orientation to ENG201 / Reviewing the course syllabus and booklet

Hafta 2: Reading a text and watching a video/listening to an audio recording to identify and evaluate main ideas, details, and arguments; distinguishing explicit and implicit meaning; participating in discussions and expressing ideas and arguments using newly learned vocabulary

Hafta 3: Reading a text and watching a video/listening to an audio recording to identify and evaluate main ideas, details, and arguments; participating in discussions and expressing ideas and arguments using newly learned vocabulary; developing and practicing well-structured oral reflection skills

Hafta 4: Reading a text to identify and evaluate main ideas, details, and arguments; taking structured notes while listening and critically evaluating the speaker's argument; synthesizing information from multiple sources (a video/an audio recording and a reading text); developing and practicing well-structured oral reflection skills; participating in discussions and expressing ideas and arguments using newly learned vocabulary

Hafta 5: Watching a video/listening to an audio recording to identify and evaluate main ideas, details, and arguments; using note-taking/marking strategies to identify and organize main arguments and supporting details in a reading text; participating in discussions and expressing ideas and arguments using newly learned vocabulary; examining a sample reflective essay to analyze its structure and components

Hafta 6: Watching a video/listening to an audio recording to identify and evaluate main ideas, details, and arguments; critically analyzing the video/audio recording to find implicit meanings; using note-taking/marking strategies to identify main arguments and details in an academic reading text; participating in discussions to express ideas; developing and practicing writing a well-structured reflective essay

Hafta 7: Reading a text and watching a video/listening to an audio recording to identify and evaluate main ideas, details, and arguments; participating in discussions and expressing opinions and arguments using newly learned vocabulary; synthesizing information from multiple sources; developing and practicing writing a well-structured reflective essay

Hafta 8: Watching a video on an academic topic to identify and evaluate ideas, details, and arguments; participating in discussions and expressing opinions and arguments using newly learned vocabulary; examining a sample poster to analyze its structure and components

Hafta 9: Critically analyzing an academic text and identifying analogies; watching a video on an academic topic to identify and evaluate main ideas, details, and arguments; participating in discussions and expressing opinions and arguments using newly learned vocabulary; identifying key components and steps in preparing and delivering an effective poster presentation (functional language and content)

Hafta 10: Reading a business-related text and watching a business-related video/listening to an audio recording to identify and evaluate ideas, details, and arguments; participating in discussions and expressing opinions and arguments using newly learned vocabulary; identifying key components and steps in preparing and delivering an effective poster presentation (body language)

Hafta 11: Reading a business-related text to identify and evaluate ideas, details, and arguments; identifying implicit meaning; evaluating components and functional language in an academic text; participating in class discussions to express opinions and arguments

Hafta 12: Watching a business-related video or listening to an audio recording to identify and evaluate ideas, details, and arguments; participating in discussions and expressing opinions and arguments using newly learned vocabulary
Hafta 13: Analyzing the poster presentation guidelines and assessment criteria to clarify content, delivery, preparation steps, and evaluation standards
Hafta 14: Delivering a well-organized, visually supported, and effectively communicated poster presentation
Hafta 15: Delivering a well-organized, visually supported, and effectively communicated poster presentation

Materials prepared by the DML instructors

courses.name: Data Structures
courses.purpose: This course introduces abstract concepts useful in problem solving and explains how these concepts are used in programming. Students learn how to choose the appropriate data structure for a specific problem and how to create complex data structures.

Use linked lists, stacks, queues, and binary trees
Apply recursion
Apply searching, sorting, and hashing
Select the appropriate data structure for a given problem
Create complex data types using existing data structures

courses.name: AKTS - Akademik İngilizce IV
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students continuing their undergraduate education in English with the ability to write a research report at B2 level according to the Common European Framework of Reference, using appropriate method (APA), organization, language, discussion, and reasoning skills. It also aims to develop students’ basic presentation skills so that they can present the report orally.
courses.description: Effective research, reporting, and presentation skills; gathering and organizing information; producing a report using appropriate techniques; delivering written and oral informative or persuasive presentations; using correct citation techniques and visuals in the process; and, when necessary, using the Flipped Learning Model.
learning_outcomes: Examine a research report to understand its specific characteristics.
Choose and narrow down a topic for writing a research report.
Research and evaluate appropriate and reliable sources.
Gain awareness of basic presentation techniques.
Prepare a detailed outline/plan for the report.
Write the introduction, body, and conclusion sections of the report.
Use in-text and end-of-text referencing appropriately.
Revise and edit the report to ensure accuracy and coherence.
Discuss the research report using basic presentation techniques.

Hafta 2: Chapter 1: Introduction to Research Report; What is research?; General information about research report; How is research done?

Hafta 3: Chapter 2: Basic Principles of Writing a Research Report; comparison of composition and research report; purpose of writing a research report; types of research reports; the process of writing a research report; topic selection; source research

Hafta 4: Chapter 2: Basic Principles of Writing a Research Report; online source research; selecting qualified sources; writing a research question; writing a thesis statement; preparing a detailed outline; outline examples
Hafta 5: Chapter 3: Citation Techniques; quoting; paraphrasing; summarizing; APA format; in-text and end-of-text referencing; language use in research reports
Hafta 6: Chapter 4: Writing Process of the Research Report; identifying the reader; elements of the research report - cover page - table of contents; writing the introduction section
Hafta 7: Worksheets on in-text/end-of-text referencing and writing the introduction
Hafta 8: Chapter 4: Writing Process of the Research Report; writing the body section; writing the conclusion section
Hafta 9: Chapter 4: Writing Process of the Research Report; writing references and appendices; writing the abstract for the report; Chapter 5: Use of Tables and Figures
Hafta 10: Feedback on the introduction section
Hafta 11: Writing the abstract, table of contents, and references

Hafta 12: Chapter 6: Oral Presentation of the Research Report; general introduction to presentation skills; requirements of a good presentation; fundamentals of presentation; audience; presentation plan; presentation; audio/visual support; preparing the oral presentation of the research report; preparing the plan of the research report; combining the sections of the oral presentation
Hafta 13: Feedback on the body and conclusion sections; presentation of the research report
Hafta 14: Presentation of the research report

Flipped Learning Course Videos (Prepared by DML instructors)

courses.name: Software Quality Assurance
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide knowledge about software quality, quality models, and the standards and methods used in the software industry, and to enable practical applications.
courses.description: Introduction to software quality and assurance. Software quality metrics. Configuration management. Review, inspection, and audit. Software process improvement models. Software reliability metrics and software quality metrics. Quality assurance standards.
learning_outcomes: Gain knowledge about the basic concepts, definitions, objectives, and activities of Software Quality Assurance (SQA) in the development and maintenance of software systems.
Discuss software quality assurance system components such as review, configuration process, and work instructions, and their practical aspects throughout the software development life cycle.
Gain knowledge about software quality management components such as quality metrics and software quality costs.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to software quality and assurance
Hafta 2: Introduction to software quality and assurance
Hafta 4: Overview of software quality assurance system components
Hafta 5: Overview of software quality assurance system components
Hafta 8: Integration of quality activities into the project life cycle
Hafta 14: Software quality assurance process standards

courses.name: Object Oriented Analysis and Design
courses.purpose: This course aims to equip students with knowledge and skills in object-oriented software analysis and design.
courses.description: Basic object-oriented concepts, object-oriented modeling with UML, object-oriented analysis and design: structural and behavioral modeling, introduction to design patterns, and application to reinforce the basic concepts.
learning_outcomes: Understand and recognize the basic concepts and principles of object-oriented analysis.
Understand and recognize the basic concepts and principles of object-oriented design.
Use object-oriented analysis and design techniques to document and develop object-oriented software.

Hafta 8: Specification of operations and control

courses.name: Database Design and Management
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to real-world database problems while focusing on the theories of database systems. Students are guided, through group work, to design and implement databases.
courses.description: Database systems concepts, data modeling with entity-relationship (ER) and extended entity-relationship (EER) models, relational data modeling, file organization and indexing structures, relational algebra, structured query language (SQL); database design: functional dependency and table normalization, introduction to database management.
learning_outcomes: Define database systems at a basic level.
Perform practical work with ER and EER models.
Design and implement relational databases.
Use SQL for data querying.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Relational Model and the Relational Database Concept
Hafta 6: Adapting ER and EER to Relational Structure
Hafta 13: Database Authorization, Security and Privacy
Hafta 14: Database Authorization, Security and Privacy

courses.name: Principles of Engineering Profession
courses.description: General introduction, team behavior and tools, needs identification, information gathering, problem definition and concept generation, decision making and concept selection, project budget preparation and analysis, project management, risk, reliability and safety, legal and ethical issues in engineering design, UN Sustainable Development Goals.

courses.name: Programming Languages
courses.purpose: To teach the concepts and paradigms of different programming languages, such as imperative, functional, and object-oriented programming. To explain how different programming concepts are handled in different types of languages. To enable students to apply what they learn through programs they develop using various programming languages.
courses.description: Programming language concepts: syntax and semantics, type concepts, values, expressions and statements; program structure, procedures and functions, structured data, abstraction and encapsulation, inheritance, dynamic binding.
learning_outcomes: Students learn the basic structures of contemporary programming languages.
Students gain the knowledge base needed to evaluate the advantages and disadvantages of different programming languages.
Students acquire the foundational knowledge required for compiler design.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Names, bindings, type checking, scopes
Hafta 5: Names, bindings, type checking, scopes
Hafta 12: Abstract data types and encapsulation

courses.name: Summer Internship I
courses.purpose: This course is intended to help students follow developments in industry. Students are required to present the experience they gain during the summer internship in a report.
courses.description: Observation work on the software and hardware parts of computer systems in use in industry for at least 6 weeks.
learning_outcomes: Recognize the industrial environment
Recognize the software used and developed in the company
Gain experience in projects involving real problems in industry
Prepare a report based on the experience gained during the summer practice
resources: Guidelines and format of the report: http://se.atilim.edu.tr/courserel/proj_guide.html

courses.name: System Software Testing and Validation
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce the fundamental principles and practices needed to ensure the quality and reliability of software systems. Through a combination of theoretical concepts and practical exercises, students learn to design, apply, and execute various test strategies to detect and correct errors in software applications.
courses.description: Software testing process and fundamentals, risk management, verification and validation, static verification and validation techniques and their comparisons, software testing strategies and techniques, software testing tools, configuration management, and software measurement and metrics related to testing.
learning_outcomes: Understand the meaning and importance of quality in software systems
Learn testing and other methods for ensuring quality
Apply different testing strategies and techniques using tool software
Understand the importance of measurement in software cost and quality

courses.name: Software Architecture
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach software design architecture, its applications, and the general structure of programming languages used for software and critical systems.
courses.description: Introduction to software architecture, architectural life cycle, architectural creation, case studies, quality attributes, software architecture design, documentation and refactoring, architectural evaluation methods, quantitative approaches in decision-making for design architecture, software production lines, and different architectural types such as layered, model-driven, and service-oriented architectures.
learning_outcomes: Explain software design architecture, its applications, and the programming languages used for software and critical systems.
Apply and document different software techniques.
Examine software architecture solutions used in real life and analyze their successes and problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 12: Architecturally Significant Requirements and Designing the Architecture
Hafta 13: Evaluating the Architecture and Documenting the Architecture
Hafta 14: Project work in the course

courses.name: Algorithm and Optimization Methods
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students how algorithms are analyzed and designed. It also enables students to apply optimization methods to optimization problems.
courses.description: Algorithm design and analysis; mathematical complexity of algorithms; master theorem; decrease-and-conquer; divide-and-conquer; transform-and-conquer; introduction to some optimization techniques; dynamic programming; greedy technique; iterative improvement; dealing with the limitations of algorithmic power.
learning_outcomes: Measure the complexity of algorithms
Analyze and design algorithms
Apply effective algorithms to real-life problems
Analyze, design, and apply optimization methods

Hafta 2: Growth of Functions and Asymptotic Notation
Hafta 3: Solving Recurrence Relations: Substitution Method
Hafta 4: Solving Recurrence Relations: Recursion-Tree Method, Master Method
Hafta 5: Brute Force Approach and Exhaustive Search

courses.name: Occupational Health and Safety
courses.description: Introduction to occupational health and safety, principles and legislation, occupational health and safety requirements to be applied in the workplace, occupational accidents, risk assessment and OHS inspections, and a proactive approach to occupational health and safety.

courses.name: Computer Networks and Operating Systems
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the fundamentals of data communication and computer networks by focusing on widely used applications on the Internet, as well as the basics of operating systems such as processes, scheduling, synchronization, deadlock, virtual memory, and paging.
courses.description: Basic concepts of computer networks, UDP and TCP services, secure data communication, network layer addressing, segment, datagram, frame, process, scheduling algorithms, deadlock detection algorithms, pointers, memory structure, and page management algorithms.
learning_outcomes: Know data communication concepts, network protocols, and standards.
Know the OSI layered network model and layer functions.
Know the working principles of widely used Internet applications.
Know the concepts of process, thread, communication, and multitasking.
Know basic algorithms such as scheduling, deadlock detection, and synchronization tools.
Know memory structures, virtual memory, and paging concepts.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to computer networks, circuit and packet switching
Hafta 2: OSI layered network service model. Delay, loss, and communication-volume concepts
Hafta 3: Fundamentals of well-known Internet applications (HTTP, SMTP, DNS)
Hafta 4: Transport layer services (UDP, TCP). Reliability in the transport layer. Segment structure.
Hafta 5: Network layer and network layer addressing. Basics of IP routing. Datagram structure
Hafta 6: Link layer services. Ethernet frame structure. Computer network topologies. Line switches and line clusters.
Hafta 7: General review and problem solving
Hafta 8: Process and thread concepts, multitasking

Recognize the software and hardware used and developed in the company
Gain experience in projects involving real problems in industry
Take part in software/hardware projects under development

courses.name: Numerical Methods for Engineers
courses.purpose: This course is designed for engineering students. Its purpose is to present numerical methods that can be used to solve mathematical problems arising in engineering that cannot be solved analytically. The philosophy of the course is to teach how the methods work so that engineering students can develop their own computer programs.
courses.description: Solution of nonlinear equations, solution of linear systems, eigenvalues and eigenvectors, interpolation and polynomial approximations, interpolation with spline functions, least squares approximation, numerical differentiation, numerical integration.
learning_outcomes: Solve a nonlinear equation in science and engineering using the MATLAB program.
Solve a linear system in science and engineering using the MATLAB program.
Find the eigenvalues and eigenvectors of a given matrix.
Learn how to use interpolation.
Learn how to construct approximation formulas for derivatives.
Learn to approximate an integral using numerical techniques.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Preliminary topics: approximation, truncation, rounding errors in calculations.
Hafta 2: Solution of nonlinear equations; fixed point; bracketing method for finding roots.
Hafta 3: Initial approximation and convergence criterion; Newton-Raphson and Secant methods.
Hafta 4: Iteration for nonlinear systems (fixed point for systems); Newton method for systems.
Hafta 5: Solution of linear systems; upper triangular (lower triangular) linear systems; Gaussian elimination and pivoting.
Hafta 8: Iterative methods for linear systems (Jacobi / Gauss Seidel methods).
Hafta 9: Eigenvalues and eigenvectors; power method (inverse power method).
Hafta 10: Interpolation and polynomial approximation; introduction to interpolation; Lagrange approximation and Newton approximation.
Hafta 11: Curve fitting; least squares line.
Hafta 13: Numerical differentiation; approximation to derivatives; numerical differentiation formulas.
Hafta 14: Numerical integration; introduction to quadrature; composite trapezoidal and Simpson rule.
resources: J. H. Mathews, K. D. Fink, Numerical Methods Using Matlab, 4th Edition, Prentice Hall, 2004.
S. C. Chapra, Applied Numerical Methods with MATLAB for Engineers and Scientists, 3rd Edition, Mc Graw Hill Education, 2012.
A. Gilat, V. Subramaniam, Numerical Methods for Engineers and Scientists: An introduction with Applications Using MATLAB, 3rd Edition, John Wiley & Sons, Inc. 2011.

courses.name: Software Project Management and Economics
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide a general overview of software project management processes and methods.
courses.description: Introduction to software project management; overview of project planning; selection of an appropriate project approach; software effort estimation; activity planning; risk analysis and management; resource allocation; project monitoring and control; contract management; managing people and organizing teams; software quality assurance; configuration management; software project management.
learning_outcomes: Review and examine software project management knowledge areas and processes.
Use different software project management methods and tools.
Apply software engineering economics techniques to project management processes.
Discuss human resources management aspects of software projects.

courses.purpose: Öğrencilere Türk dilinin yapısını, işleyiş düzenini, zenginliğini ve diğer özelliklerini kavratarak ana dil bilinci ve sevgisi kazandırmak; kişisel ve akademik gelişim süreçlerine bilinç kazandırmak; Türkiye Cumhuriyeti’nin dil ve kültür mirası hakkında farkındalık oluşturmak.

courses.name: Capstone Project
courses.purpose: This course aims to develop a project in team work in accordance with project development principles, and to test, report, and present it.
courses.description: Project analysis and design, project development, teamwork, project management, project reporting, and presenting the final product.
learning_outcomes: Plan, design, and develop a product in a team environment
Design a product according to user/system requirements
Develop the product according to a predetermined schedule and requirements
Apply relevant tests for product verification/validation
Report and explain results and findings in an integrated manner

courses.name: Practical Machine Learning
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to build an end-to-end process for data-driven problems in the context of information systems engineering, including data collection, preprocessing, visualization/analysis, modeling, and testing/evaluation. It also aims to help students select appropriate algorithms and performance metrics, develop generalizable models using cross-validation and hyperparameter optimization, and communicate findings effectively through technical reports and presentations.
courses.description: Data collection, data preprocessing, data visualization and analysis, feature engineering, machine learning algorithms, machine learning model selection and training, model testing and evaluation, hyperparameter optimization, cross-validation.
learning_outcomes: Collects data from different sources and evaluates data types and quality to create a suitable dataset for analysis.
Performs exploratory data analysis (EDA) and uses appropriate visualizations to identify variable relationships, outliers, and data issues.
Applies preprocessing (missing values, encoding, scaling) and feature engineering; justifies data representation decisions affecting model performance.
Builds, trains, and interprets basic supervised learning models for regression and classification problems.
Uses unsupervised learning (clustering/dimensionality reduction) to examine data structure and discusses the suitability of the chosen method for the data and objectives.
Compares models using cross-validation and hyperparameter search strategies; makes model selection decisions while considering the risk of test data leakage.
Selects appropriate performance metrics; performs error analysis and explains the model's strengths/weaknesses and the bias-variance trade-off.

Designs a reproducible end-to-end machine learning workflow (data-model-evaluation) for an information systems problem scenario and presents the outputs through a technical report/presentation with awareness of ethics and standards.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Course introduction; machine learning lifecycle; data-model-evaluation process
Hafta 2: Data sources; data collection; data quality; basic ethics/standards awareness
Hafta 3: Data preprocessing I: missing values, outliers, data types
Hafta 4: Exploratory Data Analysis and Visualization; variable relationships; introduction to data leakage risks
Hafta 5: Feature engineering; scaling and encoding; pipelines
Hafta 6: Supervised learning I: regression; error metrics; basic regularization
Hafta 7: Supervised learning II: classification; confusion matrix; ROC-AUC, F1
Hafta 8: Midterm exam; project problem and dataset approval
Hafta 9: Trees and ensembles (RF/boosting); overfitting discussion
Hafta 10: Unsupervised learning: clustering; dimensionality reduction (PCA)
Hafta 11: Model evaluation: cross-validation; leakage-aware design
Hafta 12: Hyperparameter optimization: grid/random; nested ideas
Hafta 13: Error analysis; metric selection; interpretability and report writing
Hafta 14: Application example: prediction/segmentation/anomaly in information systems; pre-deployment checklist
Hafta 15: Project presentations; peer feedback; general evaluation
resources: An Introduction to Statistical Learning (official website provides access to R and Python editions)
The Elements of Statistical Learning (official author website provides access to the PDF)
Introduction to Machine Learning (MIT Press book page)

courses.name: Rapid Application Development
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students user interface design and the art of visual programming using one of the industry-standard rapid application development tools. It introduces students to applications in a high-productivity development environment and exemplifies feature sets and helper tools in such environments through hands-on use of the tools. The rapid application development tool is determined by the instructor.
courses.description: Introduction to the basic language of the rapid application development tool; object definitions, methods, properties and inheritance; form design using visual components; application development using the libraries of an industry-standard rapid application development tool.
learning_outcomes: Recognize rapid application development concepts in the use of components
Distinguish the importance of related technologies
Explain client/server application development
Use rapid application development tools
Distinguish object-oriented languages from object-based languages
Explain how web services are developed and used

Hafta 2: Introduction to the rapid application development environment
Hafta 3: Introduction to the rapid application development environment
Hafta 4: Introduction to the rapid application development environment
Hafta 7: Database design and modeling tools
Hafta 8: Database design and modeling tools
Hafta 11: Application deployment flexibility and localization
Hafta 12: Application deployment wizard and tool extensibility
Hafta 13: Project with rapid application development
Hafta 14: Project with rapid application development

courses.name: User Interface Analysis and Design
courses.purpose: The course aims to teach students the theory required for high-quality user interface design and to provide the skills needed for user interface design and evaluation. It also emphasizes the importance of working with users during the user interface development process.
courses.description: User interface fundamentals, requirements gathering techniques, user, task and environment analyses, conceptual design, interaction design, design principles, interaction methods, graphical interface design, web interface design, and user interface evaluation (testing).
learning_outcomes: Emphasize the importance of user interface development
Gather user interface requirements

courses.name: Cloud Computing and Virtualization

Gain the knowledge and skills required to create and use multiple virtual machines in cloud or physical machine environments.
Explain the security implications of cloud computing and virtualization.

courses.name: Special Topics in Computer Networks
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide advanced knowledge on TCP/IP network application details and fundamental principles by focusing on widely used applications on the Internet today, and to examine technical details.
courses.description: TCP/IP network operation details, advanced technical structure of commonly used Internet applications, IPv6 addressing, routing principles, algorithms and protocols, ICMP communication, virtual private networks (VPNs), wireless networks, and network security.
learning_outcomes: Interpret and discuss the basic concepts of the TCP/IP protocol suite and data communication principles.
Explain the detailed technical service structure and installation principles of widely used Internet applications.
Understand the OSI layered network model and define the functions of the TCP/IP protocol suite according to the OSI model.
Define routing mechanisms and protocols for TCP/IP networks.
Explain IPv6 addressing and its functional use.
Identify security requirements for computer networks.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to computer networks. What is the Internet? OSI layered network architecture model.
Hafta 2: Summary of TCP/IP network operating principles. Layer functions. Segment, datagram, and frame structures.
Hafta 3: HTTP protocol summary, message format. Cookies and cookie usage. Proxy, cache control, conditional GET, content encoding.
Hafta 4: HTTP services for applications. HTTPS service. FTP protocol summary. Email protocols summary. Mail message format and MIME.
Hafta 5: DNS service and DNS protocol summary. Reverse DNS lookup. DNS poisoning. End-to-end communication protocols. End-to-end file distribution. Distributed hash table (DHT).
Hafta 6: Transport layer function summary. UDP/TCP transport services. Connection-oriented and connectionless communication. TCP and communication reliability.
Hafta 7: TCP flow and congestion control mechanisms. ATM ABR congestion control mechanism.
Hafta 8: Datagram networks and IP addressing principles summary. Routing algorithms. Link-state and distance-vector routing algorithms.
Hafta 9: Hierarchical routing. Intra-domain and inter-domain autonomous routing system. RIP, OSPF, BGP protocols. Broadcasting and multicasting.
Hafta 10: IPv6 features, services, and datagram format. IPv6-IPv4 transition systems and compatibility.

courses.name: E-Commerce - A Managerial Perspective
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide an in-depth evaluation of strategy formulation in the new economy. It also seeks to teach multi-step processes involving market and business models as well as technical issues such as design and development, together with some social issues.
courses.description: Introduction to e-business and e-commerce, electronic marketplaces, technical perspectives and infrastructure of e-commerce, e-commerce business models, current technological state, online marketing, legal environment in e-commerce, financial environment in e-commerce; introduction to enabling technologies and standards; workflow systems for e-commerce; current B2B e-commerce frameworks.
learning_outcomes: Demonstrate understanding of current and future main network standards, their historical development, and potential directions of evolution.
Demonstrate understanding of the concept of information coding, including its applications in general and specific markup languages.
Examine consumer and market behavior.
Examine other related topics such as e-commerce standards, metadata standards, search technologies, and agent technologies.
Examine how e-commerce affects consumers, other business organizations, and employees, and how business organizations currently work with consumers online.
Analyze the immediate responses of the Internet in business and see how the Internet affects global trade.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to e-commerce, Chapter 1 (main textbook)
Hafta 2: E-marketplace: mechanisms, tools, and effects of e-commerce
Hafta 8: Web 2.0 environment and social networking
Hafta 9: Workflow systems for electronic commerce. E-commerce technologies.
Hafta 10: Electronic Commerce Payment System and business generation models, e-commerce infrastructures
resources: E-Commerce 2010: A managerial Perspective, International Version, 6/E, Turban, E. and King, D., ISBN-13: 9780137034659

courses.name: Secure Software Development
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to teach the fundamental concepts of security and their role in software development. Risk management, threat models, common vulnerabilities, and the integration of security into the software development process will be identified, explored, discussed, and evaluated.
courses.description: Security fundamentals. Secure software development. DevSecOps. Risk management. Threat modeling. Data, communication, and application security. Security controls. API security. Security scenarios. Secure coding practices. OWASP Top 10. MITRE ATT&CK. Security assessment and testing. Security frameworks and guidelines.
learning_outcomes: Explain the fundamental and main elements related to security.
Explain when and how current software vulnerabilities are used in software development processes.
Discuss the possible uses of risk management and threat models.
Discuss the use of security practices, tools, and methods in software development processes.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Web resources for vulnerabilities, architecturally prioritized requirements
Hafta 14: Important issues related to ensuring security after deployment and during maintenance
resources: Course Notes and online resources will be provided.

courses.name: Introduction to Machine Learning
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the concepts and algorithms of machine learning and their applications in engineering without requiring advanced calculus, linear algebra, or probability theory.
courses.description: Artificial intelligence, machine learning, supervised and unsupervised learning, binary classification, multiclass classification, prediction, clustering, and measuring model performance.
learning_outcomes: Define the basic concepts and algorithms of machine learning and their applications.
Explain commonly used machine learning algorithms in practice.
Explain the advantages and disadvantages of machine learning algorithms.
Measure the performance of machine learning models and tune their parameters.
Apply machine learning algorithms to specific engineering problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Why machine learning? First example application: classification of the Iris flower
Hafta 2: Supervised learning: classification and prediction
Hafta 6: Decision trees and random forests
Hafta 10: Dimensionality reduction: Principal Component Analysis (PCA)
Hafta 13: Measuring model performance: cross-validation, leave-one-out, grid search
resources: Introduction to Machine Learning with Python, A Guide for Data Scientists by Andreas C. Müller and Sarah Guido, O’Reilly Media, Inc, October 2016
Machine Learning 101, Data Science. Nov 26, 2018
Hands-on machine learning with Scikit-Learn and TensorFlow: concepts, tools, and techniques to build intelligent systems / Aurelien Geron.
Introduction to Machine Learning, Ethem Alpaydin. MIT Press, 2014.

courses.name: Occupational Health and Safety
courses.purpose: To provide knowledge about occupational health and safety legislation and fundamental principles, and to educate individuals who can produce analytical solutions, apply them in practice, protect working conditions and employee health, and make recommendations in this area.
courses.description: Introduction to occupational health and safety, its principles and legislation, occupational health and safety requirements to be implemented in the workplace, occupational accidents, risk assessment and OHS inspections, and a proactive approach to occupational health and safety.
learning_outcomes: The student will gain practical knowledge about occupational health and safety.
The student will be able to produce analytical solutions regarding occupational health and safety in working life.
The student will be familiar with national legislation on occupational health and safety.
The student will gain the knowledge to take proactive approaches in working life from an occupational health and safety perspective.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Occupational Health and Safety Fundamental Principles
Hafta 2: Occupational Health and Safety Legislation
Hafta 3: Occupational Health and Safety Legislation
Hafta 4: Risk Factors / Physical, Chemical, Biological, Ergonomic, Electrical, Psychosocial
Hafta 6: Occupational Accident and Occupational Disease Management
Hafta 12: Accident Investigation, Examination and Safety Systems
Hafta 13: Occupational Health and Safety Audit Management
resources: Phil Hughes, Ed Ferrett, Introduction Health And Safety At Work, Elsevier, 2005.
John RIDLEY, Health And Safety in Brief, Elsevier, 2008.
AC van der VYVER, Risk Management in The Mining Industry, University of Pretoria, 2011
Occupational Health and Safety Law No. 6331 and Regulations

courses.name: Wind Energy Technologies
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the fundamentals of wind energy and wind energy systems, introduce the basic parameters of wind turbines, and develop the ability to work effectively in intra-disciplinary and multidisciplinary teams.
courses.description: Wind characteristics, wind energy, wind turbines, design, planning and economics of wind turbines, wave energy and wave energy conversion systems.
learning_outcomes: Understands the basic principles of wind and wave energy.
Comprehends why these energy sources are needed and their areas of use.
Gains knowledge about wind turbine design parameters.
Acquires the ability to work effectively in intra-disciplinary and multidisciplinary teams.
weekly_topics: Hafta 6: Aerodynamics of Wind Turbine Blades
Hafta 9: Measurement of Wind Speed and Direction
Hafta 10: Evaluation, Planning, Installation and Operation of Wind Projects
Hafta 12: Wave Energy and Wave Energy Conversion Systems

courses.name: Binalarda Enerji Sistemleri
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, öğrencilere bir binadaki enerji üretimini ve tüketimini verimli bir şekilde analiz etmeyi öğretmektir. Ayrıca bina yüzeylerindeki ısı kayıpları, bu kayıpların azaltılması, bina içi iklimlendirme ve elektrikli alet ile yakıt seçimi gibi konularda temel bilgi kazandırmayı hedefler.
courses.description: Bina yapısı, ısıtma-havalandırma-iklimlendirme sistemleri, yüzeylerdeki ısı kayıplarının hesaplanması ve yalıtımı, binalarda su, akaryakıt ve elektrik tüketimi, binalarda enerji verimliliği üzerine ulusal ve uluslararası standartlar ve yönetmelikler.
learning_outcomes: Bina zarfını ve bina zarfının enerji verimliliği konusundaki önemini kavramak
Binalardaki ısı kaybının kaynaklarını öğrenmek ve bunların yalıtımını sağlamak
Isıtma-havalandırma-iklimlendirme sistemlerinin binalara uygulanmasını öğrenmek
Binalardaki su, akaryakıt ve elektrik tüketiminin analizini yapabilmek
Binalardaki enerji verimliliği konusunda ulusal ve uluslararası standartlar hakkında bilgi sahibi olmak

Hafta 7: Isıtma, havalandırma ve iklimlendirme sistemleri
Hafta 8: Isıtma, havalandırma ve iklimlendirme sistemleri

courses.name: Project Management
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students how to initiate, develop, calculate, and implement projects efficiently and effectively. It helps students understand the relationship between project personnel and project success, and develop a solid control over projects by recognizing when and why a project deviates from the plan and whether the plan should be revised more realistically. It also suggests ways to avoid risks and conflicts.
courses.description: Elements and phases of project management; functions of project management (planning, staffing, scheduling, tracking and control) and techniques (CPM, PERT); software tools for project management; project cost control and time/resource management; leadership types, conflict and risk management.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to provide a systematic approach to project planning, performance, and control.
Students will understand the importance of leadership approaches and inter-team cooperation in achieving solid control over a project.
Students will be able to distinguish plans related to developing a method for transforming an idea into a product or service.
Students will interpret the importance of teamwork in successful project management.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Organizational Influences in Project Management
Hafta 3: Project Life Cycle and Processes
Hafta 4: Project Selection and Portfolio Management
Hafta 5: Leadership, Project Management, Team Building
Hafta 8: Project Planning: Networks, Duration Estimation and Critical Path
Hafta 10: Project Planning: Networks, Duration Estimation and Critical Path
Hafta 13: Project Evaluation and Control Exam
resources: Jeffrey K. Pinto, Project Management, Pearson, 2016
A Guide to the Project Management Body of Knowledge 6e, PMI, 2017

courses.name: Biomaterials
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students biomaterial behavior, toxicology, and biocompatibility, as well as the properties, performance, and application areas of biomaterials.
courses.description: Definition of biomaterials, biocompatibility, host response, synthetic and biological materials, classification of synthetic biomaterials, polymers, factors affecting implants and host system, application areas of biomaterials, history of implants, current studies and trends in biomaterials, bulk and mechanical properties of materials, surface analysis methods, metallic, polymeric, ceramic and composite biomaterials, sterilization methods for extracorporeal devices.
learning_outcomes: Students who successfully complete this course will acquire the necessary knowledge about the properties, performance, and application areas of biomaterials at a level usable by biomengineers, materials scientists, and physicians.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definition of biomaterials, biocompatibility and host system
Hafta 15: Additional class before final exam (study)
resources: Biomaterials An Introduction, Joon Park, R.S. Lakes, 3rd Edition, Springer, 2007.
Biomaterials Principles and Applications, Joon Park, Joseph D. Bronzino, CRC Press, 2003.
Biomaterials and Bioengineering Handbook, Donald L. Wiss, 2003.
Biomaterials in the Design and Reliability of Medical Devices, Michael N. Helmus, Eurekah, 2002.

courses.name: Solar Energy Technologies

courses.description: Conversion of solar energy, fundamentals of solar radiation, methods of collecting solar energy and thermal cycle, solar heating systems, solar thermal power generation, and obtaining solar energy using biomass.
learning_outcomes: Gain theoretical and practical knowledge about solar energy.
Learn the methods used in energy conversion analysis in solar energy applications.
Develop the ability to work effectively in intra-disciplinary and multidisciplinary teams.

Hafta 2: Solar Energy and Usable Solar Radiation, Solar Angles, Solar Radiation Reaching the Earth Surface and Solar Radiation Outside the Atmosphere

Hafta 4: Methods of Collecting Solar Energy and Thermal Cycle
Hafta 9: Storage of Thermal Energy and Use of Thermal Energy in Power Generation
Hafta 11: Solar Cells and Direct Conversion of Solar Energy into Electrical Energy
Hafta 12: Solar Cells and Direct Conversion of Solar Energy into Electrical Energy, Photovoltaic System Design

courses.name: Rapid Prototyping
courses.purpose: This course introduces students to rapid prototyping and automated fabrication technologies, along with the CAD models and material science foundations needed to use them effectively. It also aims to show the practical design and fabrication of a real part and to discuss the impact of these technologies on society.
courses.description: Rapid prototyping technologies, CAD models suitable for automated manufacturing, secondary processing methods, additive manufacturing technologies, stereolithography, FDM, laminated object manufacturing, selective laser sintering, direct metal laser sintering, casting methods for rapid prototyping, precision casting, rapid tooling, and reverse engineering.
learning_outcomes: Explain current rapid prototyping systems, their basic operating principles, and characteristics.
Explain the complementary secondary fabrication processes commonly used with the rapid prototyping systems described above.
Select the appropriate fabrication technology or technologies for a given prototyping task.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to rapid prototyping and automated fabrication technologies • What is a prototype? • Why prototype? • What is automated fabrication? • History of numerical control • Process planning; manual, variable and generative
Hafta 2: Introduction to injection molding • Introduction to injection molding • Injection molding design • Material selection • UL standards

Hafta 3: Rapid prototyping technologies • Machine tool motion • History of layered manufacturing • Stereolithography • Solid ground curing • Selective laser sintering • Fused deposition modeling • Laminated object manufacturing • Other systems

Hafta 4: Rapid prototyping technologies • Machine tool motion • History of layered manufacturing • Stereolithography • Solid ground curing • Selective laser sintering • Fused deposition modeling • Laminated object manufacturing • Other systems
Hafta 5: Underlying material science • Photopolymers • Thermoplastics • Powders

Hafta 7: Producing models suitable for automated fabrication • .STL file format • Repairing CAD models • Adding support structures • Model separation
Hafta 8: Producing models suitable for automated fabrication • .STL file format • Repairing CAD models • Adding support structures • Model separation
Hafta 9: Secondary application process • RTV silicone rubber molding • Investment casting • Improving prototyping quality • Improving production • Medical applications
Hafta 10: Secondary application process • RTV silicone rubber molding • Investment casting • Improving prototyping quality • Improving production • Medical applications
